Why is it an approximate day? Because if I put you or any other human being who have a normal circadian clock in a constant conditions with no information about feeding time about sleep time about what time it is outside you still have a daily rhythm but it's not exactly 24 hours. So it will shift out of the solar day because it's not exactly 24 hours and hence the name sir. >> How does that rhythm show up in the tissues of our body? >> It shows up at every level that we know we studied. I think it's one of the strongest aspect of survival for animals to have the anticipation and the adjustment to the solar cycle. >> What is the the machinery that allows that to happen and how does that machinery work? Yeah. So we know we knew that in mammals including us we are mammals humans that the eyes are required for this function. So if humans are born without eyes or the optic nerves are damaged humans are not able to adjust to the solar cycle. So we know that the eyes are required in the human retinas.
